accepted.reason: Vector geometry primitives (orientation, nearest-point, bbox/hash utilities) are strong standalone computational kernels.
Run-length style packing/unpacking of numeric arrays is a practical performance-oriented data transformation kernel.
Sudoku unique-candidate scan across row/col/block areas is deterministic grid logic with clear optimization structure.
Subsequence enumeration and cumulative valuation are useful combinatorial kernels for algorithmic trace diversity.
Alias-table initialization and O(1) categorical sampling are high-value probabilistic algorithm kernels.

skipped.reason: Cython type error behavior test, not kernel training content.
Docstring semantics test module with no performance-kernel signal.
Heavily tied to SciPy LAPACK bindings and external numeric stack; not a clean standalone conversion example.
Mostly wrapper/interop code around external C++ conversion classes rather than reusable compute kernels.
Prime/factorization patterns are already represented in the current unpaired set, so this would be low-diversity duplication.
